cards.front: What is the p-value used for in statistical hypothesis testing?
cards.back: To determine the significance of the results and whether the null hypothesis can be rejected

cards.front: What is the difference between a t-test and an ANOVA test?
cards.back: A t-test compares two groups, while ANOVA compares multiple groups

cards.front: What is multiple testing, and how does it impact statistical inference?
cards.back: Multiple testing refers to conducting multiple statistical tests on the same data. It can lead to false positives due to increased Type I error rates

cards.front: What is the concept of degrees of freedom in statistical tests?
cards.back: Degrees of freedom refer to the number of values in a dataset that are free to vary, often used to calculate the p-value and determine statistical significance

cards.front: What is the purpose of adjusting p-values for multiple testing?
cards.back: To reduce the risk of Type I errors due to multiple comparisons, often using methods like Bonferroni correction or Holm-Bonferroni method
